co_occurrences.evidence: Type 2 diabetes and essential hypertension co-occur in 60-70% of cases (Elixhauser comorbidity index)
Congestive heart failure and atrial fibrillation co-occur in 40-50% of heart failure patients
COPD exacerbation with concurrent pneumonia is common trigger for acute exacerbation
Acute MI complicated by heart failure occurs in 15-25% of MI presentations
Diabetic nephropathy is a leading cause of CKD; 30-40% of diabetics develop CKD
Major depressive disorder and generalized anxiety disorder co-occur in 50-60% of cases
Sepsis frequently secondary to bacterial pneumonia; pneumonia is leading sepsis source
Breast cancer with axillary lymph node metastasis occurs in 30-40% of invasive cases
Bilateral knee osteoarthritis and chronic low back pain commonly co-occur in elderly
Diabetic foot ulcer is a common complication of poorly controlled type 2 diabetes
Hypertension and hyperlipidemia co-occur in metabolic syndrome; shared cardiovascular risk
Hypertensive nephrosclerosis is second leading cause of CKD after diabetes
Diabetic neuropathy and retinopathy commonly co-occur as microvascular complications
Chronic systolic heart failure with chronic atrial fibrillation is a frequent combination
Type 2 diabetes and hyperlipidemia are core components of metabolic syndrome
COPD patients frequently have comorbid hypertension from shared smoking risk factors
CKD patients have higher prevalence of hypothyroidism due to impaired thyroid hormone clearance
Pulmonary embolism commonly originates from deep vein thrombosis (Virchow triad)
Peripheral vascular disease is common macrovascular complication of type 2 diabetes
Hemiplegia and aphasia commonly co-occur as sequelae of cerebral infarction

conflicts.reason: Cannot code both Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes mellitus (Excludes1: E10 excludes E11 and vice versa)
Acute STEMI of anterior wall (I21.0) excludes old myocardial infarction (I25.2) on same encounter
Acute MI unspecified (I21.9) excludes old MI (I25.2) when coding the acute event
Mild intermittent asthma (J45.20) excludes COPD unspecified (J44.9) — use J44.9 with asthma codes from J45.9x
DM type 2 without complications (E11.9) excludes DM type 2 with hyperglycemia (E11.65) — use the more specific code
DM type 1 without complications (E10.9) excludes DM type 1 with hyperglycemia (E10.65) — use the more specific code
DM type 2 without complications (E11.9) excludes DM type 2 with neuropathy (E11.40) — use the more specific code
DM type 2 without complications (E11.9) excludes DM type 2 with retinopathy (E11.311) — use the more specific code
DM type 2 without complications (E11.9) excludes DM type 2 with foot ulcer (E11.621) — use the more specific code
Left ventricular failure (I50.1) excludes heart failure unspecified (I50.9) — use the more specific code
Chronic systolic heart failure (I50.22) excludes heart failure unspecified (I50.9) — use the more specific code
Moderate persistent asthma with acute exacerbation (J45.41) excludes mild intermittent asthma (J45.20)
CKD stage 3 (N18.3) excludes CKD unspecified (N18.9) — use the more specific stage code
Recurrent MDD moderate (F33.1) excludes single episode MDD moderate (F32.1) — these are mutually exclusive episode types
Acute STEMI of anterior wall (I21.0) excludes acute MI unspecified (I21.9) — use the more specific code

qualifier_requirements.rationale: Diabetic neuropathy requires specification of neuropathy type and affected side
Diabetic retinopathy requires laterality (right/left/bilateral) and severity (mild/moderate/severe)
Acute MI unspecified should specify wall involvement (anterior/inferior/lateral) and initial/subsequent
Heart failure unspecified should specify systolic/diastolic/combined and acute/chronic
COPD with acute exacerbation should document severity of exacerbation
CKD stage 3 is already specified but should document 3a vs 3b substage
Breast cancer requires laterality specification for accurate coding
Asthma with acute exacerbation should document severity classification
Lumbar disc herniation should specify intervertebral disc level
Pulmonary embolism should specify acute vs chronic and with/without cor pulmonale
Chronic systolic heart failure should document NYHA functional class
Sepsis unspecified should document causative organism and severity (severe/septic shock)
Hypothyroidism should specify primary vs secondary and congenital vs acquired
Recurrent MDD should specify severity and presence/absence of psychotic features
